LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DESOTO CAB COMPANY INC.S MOTION FOR ORDER STRIKING PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT - THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES PURSUANT TO CCP 1021.5. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE AT THIS STAGE OF THE PROCEEDINGS WHETHER SUCH FEES ARE RECOVERABLE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE BACKGROUND INFORMATION ALLEGATIONS IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT IRRELEVANT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 436(A). THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS CONCEDED. ORDER SIGNED. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attoenry, Cody D. Knight of Knight Employment Law (310.444.3039) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Loren Kieve, Esq. (415.364.0060) appears as counsel for defendants/demurring parties, SF Taxi Co., Jilcha Sambi and Comfort Cab

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court indicates it may recommend this action be singly assigned for all purposes in the interest of justice. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS JILCHA SAMBI, S.F. TAXI-CAB CO., COMFORT CAB, INC.'S (ANTI-SLAPP) MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action alleged in the complaint is DENIED. Neither of the causes of action alleged by plaintiff Richard Hybels arises from any protected activity and thus defendants have not satisfied their burden on the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. The allegations concerning protected activity in the complaint are at most incidental to the two causes of action and merely proved context to plaintiff's salient allegations. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attoenry, Cody D. Knight of Knight Employment Law (310.444.3039) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Loren Kieve, Esq. (415.364.0060) appears as counsel for defendants/demurring parties, SF Taxi Co., Jilcha Sambi and Comfort Cab

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court indicates it may recommend this action be singly assigned for all purposes in the interest of justice. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS JILCHA SAMBI, S.F. TAXI-CAB CO., COMFORT CAB, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. The two causes of action alleged by plaintiff Richard Hybels are neither uncertain nor plead insufficient facts to support the relief sought. While the use of the term "defendants" in a complaint without differentiating among them sometimes makes a pleading uncertain, that is not the case here. All named defendants are identically situated per the allegations of the complaint and thus no useful purpose is served by repetitious allegations that each defendant by name acted in the way that all defendants collectively are alleged to have acted. The specificity required for pleading a fraud claim is not applicable. The complaint adequately alleges that each defendant employs drivers who are entitled to be but are not covered under workers compensation insurance policies. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DESOTO CAB COMPANY INC.S Motion For Order Striking Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint - The motion to strike is denied as to the request for attorney's fees pursuant to CCP 1021.5. The court cannot determine at this stage of the proceedings whether such fees are recoverable. The motion to strike as to the background information allegations is denied. The allegations are not irrelevant within the meaning of CCP 436(a). The remainder of the motion is granted without leave to amend as conceded. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Continued To Jun-27-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ilya Filmus of Infinity Law Group telephone number 415-426-3580 for Plainitff Richard Hybels.
Joe Bautista of Seibert & Bautista telephone number 415-954-9455 for Defendant Desoto Cab Company.

Mr. Filmus stated that he did not receive any notice of the motion until yesterday evening and requested a continuance. Mr. Bautista objects to a continuance and requests sanctions. The Court rules that plaintiff has presented sufficient cause to continue the matter and will not issue sanctions. The tentative ruling is vacated. DEFENDANT DESOTO CAB COMPANY INC.S' Motion For Order Striking Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint is continued to June 27, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302. Responsive papers are to be filed pursuant to code.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)
